name: United Valley Bank
description_short: Community-focused bank with 9 locations across North Dakota and Minnesota, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and business services with local decision-making.
description_long:
United Valley Bank is a regional community bank operating across the Upper Midwest with roots in Grand Forks, North Dakota and surrounding areas. The bank has expanded to nine locations including Grand Forks, Cavalier, Argyle, Hallock, Lancaster, Mahnomen, Twin Valley, Crookston, and a newly opened Fargo branch (opened February 4, 2026). The bank emphasizes its local heritage and community connections as central to its business model, positioning itself as an alternative to larger national banks.

United Valley Bank offers a full range of consumer and business banking products. These include checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), mortgage lending for home purchases and refinancing, business lines of credit, cash management solutions, and mobile banking services. The bank provides online mortgage applications and operates both lobby and drive-up services at its branches. They also offer mobile wallet integration and custom mobile alerts for account management.

The bank differentiates itself through its emphasis on local decision-making and community understanding. Marketing materials stress that their "roots are in the communities they serve" and position the bank as one that understands local business and agricultural needs. The bank has invested in expansion to new markets like Fargo, hiring dedicated local staff to bring "fresh banking products and energy" to new areas. The company recently promoted four staff members to key executive positions.

While United Valley Bank offers competitive standard banking products, consumers should note the bank's geographic limitation to the Upper Midwest region. The bank operates with standard business hours (Monday-Friday 8AM-5PM, Saturday drive-up 9AM-12PM) and requires a $500 minimum deposit for certain products. As a regional community bank, it may lack some of the digital-first features or broader product offerings of larger national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Community-focused bank with 9 locations and local decision-making authority
Full-service banking including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, mortgages, and business lending
Mobile app available with custom alerts and mobile wallet integration
Cash management solutions for business customers
Extended drive-up hours on Saturday mornings
Recently expanded to Fargo market (9th location opened February 2026)
cons: Geographic limitation to Upper Midwest (North Dakota and Minnesota only)
$500 minimum deposit requirement for account opening
Limited weekend lobby hours (drive-up only on Saturday, 9AM-12PM)
Early withdrawal penalties applied to CD products
No mention of mobile check deposit, bill pay, or other digital conveniences standard at larger banks
best_for: Small business owners and farmers in North Dakota and Minnesota seeking local lending relationships
Residents of Grand Forks, Fargo, and surrounding communities looking for community-focused banking
Agricultural customers and rural businesses requiring specialized cash management
diagnosis: United Valley Bank is best for small business owners, farmers, and residents in the Upper Midwest who prioritize community relationships and local decision-making in banking. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only operates in North Dakota and Minnesota, making it inaccessible for consumers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
